methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Rainmaker
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Intramotev
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Seneca is a Series A stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector led by Stuart Landesberg and Nick Foley. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 8 private companies in the sector, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No direct public comparables exist in this narrow space, limiting pure market-based triangulation.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable approach given data constraints. Using Seneca's $60M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression yields an implied valuation of $120M. The peer-median method was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow peer set—while 5 same-sector peers exist, none met the minimum threshold of 3 datapoints in filtered subgroups, and a broad sector median would introduce material bias.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $102M to $138M, with a median of $120M. This range reflects uncertainty bands around the single applicable method rather than convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of publicly traded comps for calibration, limited visibility into burn rate and path-to-revenue relative to peer cohorts, and the early-stage nature of the commercial drone and autonomous vehicle markets. The range is conservative absent additional financial or market data.
